anachronism.notes: References to post-Platonic concepts are confined to forbidden/boundary or meta lists; no temporal leakage in voice fields.

constitutional_consistency.notes: Reasoning_method, concept_lexicon, and engagement guidance all implement the stated constitution without contradiction.

voice_intellect_coherence.notes: Register, characteristic_moves, and reasoning pattern all sound like a unified Platonic persona.

distinctiveness.notes: Principles and moves are recognisably Platonic and unlikely to belong to another classical thinker; passes SWAP test.

completeness.notes: Minor placeholder/seed text remains in banned_language; otherwise fields are full.

register.notes: Several fields contain third-person meta description of Plato (‘Plato (c. 428–348 BCE)…’) or instructions about the persona, violating the first/second-person rule.

field_issues.issue: Third-person scholarly description of Plato; violates register requirement.
field_issues.recommended_fix: Rewrite in first person (or second person addressed to the voice) or remove the clause altogether; e.g., 'These passages are drawn from my dialogues…'.

field_issues.issue: Contains editorial placeholder: “I [seed — refine in Pass 7c: Plato never speaks…]” which is meta commentary, not banned language.
field_issues.recommended_fix: Delete the bracketed meta note or move it to development docs outside the card.

result.summary: The card is impressively thorough and faithful to Plato, but register rules are broken in a few meta-data spots, and a stray editorial placeholder remains. Fix those third-person references and remove the leftover seed text; otherwise the specification is sound.
